name: Clearscope SEO Content Optimization API
what_it_does: AI-powered SEO content optimization platform with REST API — analyzing top-ranking pages for target keywords to provide content grade scores, semantic keyword recommendations, readability metrics, and competitive gap analysis to help content teams write higher-ranking content.
use_cases: Querying Clearscope content reports to integrate SEO recommendations into CMS workflows
Automating content audit by batch-analyzing existing articles against target keywords
Pulling content grade scores for editorial dashboards and content performance tracking
Integrating Clearscope keyword recommendations into AI content generation pipelines
Syncing content performance data with marketing analytics for SEO ROI reporting
not_for: Technical SEO auditing (use Screaming Frog or Sitebulb for technical audits)
Backlink analysis and link building (use Ahrefs or Moz for links)
Keyword research without content optimization context
best_when: A content marketing team wants to systematically optimize new and existing articles for on-page SEO relevance using AI-analyzed competitor content as a benchmark.
avoid_when: You primarily need technical SEO, backlink analysis, or keyword ranking tracking rather than content optimization.

auth.notes: API key from Clearscope account settings. Passed as Authorization header (Token format). Account-level access — all reports in account accessible. No OAuth2 or scopes. No webhooks — polling for async report generation.

pricing.paid_tiers: Essentials $170/month (100 reports); Business $350/month (300 reports); Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: San Francisco-based company. Founded 2018. Bootstrapped and profitable. Focused on semantic SEO and NLP analysis. Integrates with Google Docs and WordPress editor. Used by major brands: HubSpot, Deloitte, Adobe. Report-credit pricing model. Content grader gives letter grades (A+ to F).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for reports and terms. Token auth format. Good but limited API documentation. Report generation is async — submit keyword, poll for status. Content grade and term recommendations returned after analysis completes. Report credits consumed per report — track usage.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Report reads idempotent by report ID. Term reads idempotent. New report creation consumes credits — check for existing report before creating duplicate.
known_agent_gotchas: Report creation consumes report credits — verify no existing report before creating
Report generation is async — poll status endpoint or implement delay before fetch
No webhooks — implement polling loop for report completion
SERP analysis varies by location — specify target country for localized results

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. US data residency. Token-based auth. SEO content data is business-sensitive — secure API key storage. No PII in SEO content reports — lower sensitivity than personal data tools.
